threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it’s a ‘trendy café’ if it doesn’t serve at least four types of oat milk and have paper straws.
Last night on the 36, a guy serenaded the driver with old school R&B. Only in Peckham would you get a full-on karaoke session at 3am.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I found myself sharing a bus with a musician testing their saxophone at 7 AM. Only in Neukölln do mornings sound like jazz.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a café that serves currywurst with vegan mayo and screens retro 80s films at brunch. It's a vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their reaction to the BART delays—total acceptance mixed with a side-eye for tourists who think it’s a vacation problem.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today at Civic Center, and a saxophonist had the entire crowd swaying. Only in SF do delays turn into dance breaks.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just for Gaudí. They miss the vibrant art scene in local galleries that actually define our culture.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their hesitance at the tram stops, checking schedules like they're departing from an airport.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look baffled when someone mentions the 'Bussey Building' and how it's just a bar now, not an actual bus depot.
Caught the 59 last night—two guys were arguing over who saw a ghost at the old cinema. Proper eerie vibe, but the driver just kept his cool.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I found a stray cat napping on my balcony, staring down the U-Bahn tracks like it owned the place. Now that’s Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Prenzlauer Berg do parents push strollers while sipping craft beer from a café—a true Berlin blend of family and leisure you won't find anywhere else.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local when they know the exact time the 14 bus usually breaks down by the taco truck on 24th.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a ride with a guy in full pirate gear on the 19. Turns out he was just waiting for his wife to finish her shift at the local restaurant. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the bustling street markets are for locals, but many are just visiting for the 'authentic' vibe—locals avoid the crowds.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them easily; they're the ones still taking photos of the mosaics in Plaça Viaducte while locals rush past.
